label: Save File
description: Persist arbitrary text content to the artifact store (MinIO/GCS/Azure via the platform's BlobStorageService). Returns {artifactId, url, sizeBytes, mimeType}. Text payloads only for now — binary mode lands when the binary plane (Tier 2 #5) is wired through. For string-valued flow outputs (JSON, CSV, HTML, Markdown, logs) the text path already covers the canonical use case.

settings.name: content
settings.label: Content
settings.description: The payload to persist. Strings are written verbatim; objects/arrays are JSON.stringify'd before write.

settings.name: mimeType
settings.label: MIME type
settings.description: Content type. Auto-derived from filename when omitted (.html→text/html, .json→application/json, .csv→text/csv, .md→text/markdown, else text/plain).

settings.name: title
settings.label: Title
settings.description: Human-readable title shown in the artifacts library.

settings.name: tags
settings.label: Tags
settings.description: Array of tags applied to the artifact for search/filter.

ai.shortDescription: Persist text content (HTML / JSON / CSV / Markdown / logs) to the artifact store.
ai.whenToUse: When a flow produces a report/artifact that needs a stable URL — Slack/email links, reload-survivable inspection, sharing outside the runtime envelope.
ai.examplePrompt: save the cluster-triage report as triage-report.html so the slack message can link to it
ai.promptHints: Reference output via {{steps.X.result.url}} and {{steps.X.result.artifactId}}. Filename extension drives the default MIME type.

outputAssertions.name: artifact_created
outputAssertions.errorMessage: save_file did not return an artifactId.
